Discord Notifications on Mac: Turn Them On and Tame the Server Noise
Lately a lot of services — and my own company — have moved to Discord, so I figured I'd jot down how to enable notifications on macOS.
On Mac you might not get Discord notifications at all. Sometimes you only get a little red dot.
Sometimes you don't even get the red dot.
So when a friend DMs you or some channel pings, you'll never see it. How do you fix this?
You'll see "Allow Notifications" is off. Turn it on and pick whatever notification style you like. You can copy mine if you want.
Now you'll get both channel and DM notifications.
Thanks to a student who asked me about this while I was writing the article.
You'll also see unread counts on Discord now.

Once you turn notifications on, every server you've joined is going to start pinging you for everything, which gets annoying fast. So you can mute notifications per channel or per server. I'll use the Hexschool server as an example.
Open the channel you want to mute, click the top bar, and find "Notification Settings".
You can either mute the whole channel or set it to only ping when you're @-mentioned.
If you don't even want to see the unread dot, mute the whole channel.
If you just want to silence pings, adjust "Server Notification Settings" instead.
Muting a specific channel inside a server
If you don't want to mute the whole server, you can mute a single channel by clicking into it and hitting the bell icon at the top.
Click it once and the channel's notifications toggle on/off.
As for Windows... I haven't run into this problem there, so I can't really speak to it.

Lately a lot of services and my own company have migrated to Discord, so here's a quick note on how to enable Discord notifications on macOS and tame channel-level noise once things start flooding in.
